---
title: "Greenland Glacier Retreat Susceptibility Application"
subtitle: "A Google Earth Engine App for Cryosphere–Industry Interaction"
author: "Entao Wang, Penghao Gao, XinLei Shi, Yunjie Zhou, Siyu Chen, Ho Man Chan"
institute: "Data Dishwasher CASA0025: Building Spatial Applications with Big Data"
date: "2026/04/25 (updated: `r Sys.Date()`)"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: [default, xaringan-themer.css]
    lib_dir: libs
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
    seal: false
---

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
library(fontawesome)
```

```{r xaringan-themer, include=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(xaringanthemer)

style_mono_light(
  base_color = "#1b3a4b",
  background_color = "#fafafa",
  header_font_google = google_font("Lato"),
  text_font_google   = google_font("Lato"),
  code_font_google   = google_font("Lato"),
  base_font_size = "20px",
  header_h1_font_size = "2.2rem",
  header_h2_font_size = "1.7rem",
  header_h3_font_size = "1.3rem",
  extra_css = list(
    ".remark-slide-content" = list(
      "font-size" = "20px",
      "line-height" = "1.4"
    ),
    ".title-slide" = list(
      "background-image" = "url('images/greenland_ice.webp')",
      "background-size" = "cover",
      "background-position" = "center",
      "background-repeat" = "no-repeat",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
      ),
    ".title-slide a" = list(
      "color" = "white",
      "text-decoration" = "none"
      ),
    ".title-slide a:visited" = list(
      "color" = "white"
      ),
    ".title-slide h1" = list(
      "font-size" = "44px",
      "margin-bottom" = "16px",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
    ),
    ".title-slide h2" = list(
      "font-size" = "26px",
      "margin-top" = "0px",
      "margin-bottom" = "24px",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
    ),
    ".title-slide h3" = list(
      "font-size" = "18px",
      "font-weight" = "normal",
      "margin-bottom" = "10px",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
    )
  )
)
```

```{r tech-setup, include=FALSE}
library(xaringanthemer)
library(xaringanExtra)

options(htmltools.dir.version = FALSE)

style_extra_css(css = list(
   ".pull-left-wide" = list("float" = "left", "width" = "60%"),
   ".pull-right-narrow" = list("float" = "right", "width" = "35%"),
   ".pull-left-3" = list("float" = "left", "width" = "30%"),
   ".pull-middle-3" = list("float" = "left", "width" = "30%", "margin-left" = "5%", "margin-right" = "5%"),
   ".pull-right-3" = list("float" = "right", "width" = "30%"),
   ".bottom-note" = list("position" = "absolute","bottom" = "1em","left" = "1em", "right" = "1em"),
   ".clearfix" = list("clear" = "both", "display" = "block")
))
xaringanExtra::use_panelset()
```

class: center, middle, title-slide

# Monitoring Glacier Retreat and Industrial Exposure in Greenland

## A Google Earth Engine App for Cryosphere–Industry Interaction

### Data Dishwasher 

### CASA0025: Building Spatial Applications with Big Data

### (updated: `r Sys.Date()`)

---

# Greenland Context

.pull-left-wide[
**Why Greenland?**

- Greenland is a strong case for integrated cryosphere monitoring because glacier retreat, terrain change, and industrial development can be studied together.
- The project connects glacier boundaries, mining activity, ports, shipping, elevation, and settlements in one Earth Engine workflow.
- This responds directly to the need for a platform-based approach rather than separate static layers.
]

.pull-right-narrow[
```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='100%', fig.align='center'}
knitr::include_graphics("images/greenland_mosaic.jpg")
```
<p style="font-size: 0.6em; text-align: center; margin-top: 0;">
Figure 1: Greenland glacier landscape
</p>
]

.clearfix[]

<div style="background: #1b3a4b; color: white; padding: 10px 16px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 0.9em;">
Greenland has strong glacier-monitoring potential, but the main challenge is integrating cryosphere change with industrial and infrastructure layers in one interactive platform.
</div>

---

class: inverse, center, middle


## Interface logic and layer design

---

# Main App Interface

.pull-left-wide[
**Core interface design**

- Main map for glacier extent, retreat hotspots, mining sites, ports, shipping density, terrain, and settlements
- Layer toggles for switching between cryosphere, industrial, and environmental datasets
- Click-on-map inspector for glacier change, distance to mine, distance to port, and settlement context
- Output panel for hotspot summaries and screening indicators
]

.pull-right-narrow[
```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='100%', fig.align='center'}
knitr::include_graphics("images/greenland_ice_sheet.jpg")
```
<p style="font-size: 0.6em; text-align: center; margin-top: 0;">
Figure 2: Example dashboard layout for Greenland monitoring
</p>
]

.clearfix[]

---

# Layer Controls

.pull-left[
### Cryosphere layers

- GLIMS glacier outlines
- Landsat historical extent
- Sentinel-2 recent extent
- Retreat hotspot polygons
]

.pull-right[
### Context layers

- GEUS mineral deposits
- Ports and harbour locations
- AIS shipping density
- ArcticDEM and settlements
]

---

# App Outputs

.pull-left[
### Map outputs

- Glacier extent map
- Historical-to-current retreat comparison
- Mining and port overlay
- Terrain and thermal context
]

.pull-right[
### Analytical outputs

- Distance from retreat zones to mines
- Distance from retreat zones to ports
- Settlement proximity screening
- Priority hotspot identification
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# Methodology

## Data integration and analytical workflow

---

# Methodology Overview

.panelset[
.panel[.panel-name[Step 1: Glacier Mapping]
- Use GLIMS as reference glacier boundary data
- Use Landsat for long-term comparison from 1984 onward
- Use Sentinel-2 for more recent and finer glacier mapping
]

.panel[.panel-name[Step 2: Context Layers]
- Add GEUS mineral and mining datasets
- Add port and AIS-related shipping layers
- Add ArcticDEM and settlement layers
]

.panel[.panel-name[Step 3: Overlay Analysis]
- Compare retreat zones with industrial features
- Measure proximity to mines, ports, and settlements
- Identify spatial hotspots for screening
]
]

---

# Key Datasets

| Theme | Main data source | Role in the app |
|------|------|------|
| Glacier boundary | GLIMS 2023 | Present-day glacier reference |
| Historical imagery | Landsat 5 / 8 | Long-term glacier extent comparison |
| Recent imagery | Sentinel-2 SR Harmonized | Higher-resolution recent mapping |
| Topography | ArcticDEM 2m | Elevation, slope, terrain setting |
| Thermal context | MODIS / VIIRS / Landsat ST | Melt and temperature support |
| Industrial features | GEUS, ports, AIS | Mining and transport exposure |
| Human geography | NunaGIS, Statistics Greenland | Settlement and context layers |

---

# Glacier Mapping Strategy

.pull-left[
### Boundary logic

- GLIMS provides the reference outline layer
- Landsat supports broad historical comparison
- Sentinel-2 refines recent glacier margins
]

.pull-right[
### Main output

- Retreat polygons or change areas
- Glacier hotspot map
- Spatial comparison with nearby industry
]

---

# Industrial and Exposure Layers

.pull-left[
### Industrial footprint

- Mineral deposits and mining sites
- Ports and harbour points
- Shipping activity density
]

.pull-right[
### Exposure screening

- Settlement proximity
- Distance to infrastructure
- Co-location with retreat hotspots
]

---

# Terrain and Climate Support

.pull-left[
### ArcticDEM

- Elevation
- Slope
- Aspect
- Terrain interpretation near glacier fronts
]

.pull-right[
### Thermal layers

- MODIS and VIIRS as broad thermal context
- Landsat ST as intermediate thermal support
- Contextual support for melt interpretation
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# How It Works

## From user input to hotspot output

---

# App Logic

.panelset[
.panel[.panel-name[1–2]
.pull-left[
### 1. Select period

User chooses a year or comparison range for glacier monitoring.
]

.pull-right[
### 2. Load glacier data

The app displays glacier outlines and related imagery.
]
]

.panel[.panel-name[3–4]
.pull-left[
### 3. Detect change

Retreat areas are derived from historical and recent extents.
]

.pull-right[
### 4. Overlay industry

Mining, ports, shipping, and settlements are added to the map.
]
]

.panel[.panel-name[5]
### 5. Return outputs

- Hotspot maps
- Summary indicators
- Spatial exposure patterns
]
]

---

# Example User Question

.pull-left[
### User asks

Which glacier retreat hotspots in Greenland are close to industrial activity and local settlements?
]

.pull-right[
### App returns

- Retreat polygons
- Nearby mines or ports
- Distance measures
- Screening indicators
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# Limitations

## What the platform can and cannot do

---

# Main Limitations

.pull-left[
### Technical

- Temporal mismatch across GLIMS, Landsat, Sentinel-2, ArcticDEM, VIIRS, and MODIS
- Resolution mismatch between coarse thermal data and fine terrain data
- Some external elevation-change products are not directly available in GEE
]

.pull-right[
### Interpretation

- Proximity does not prove causality
- Dust and albedo effects are better treated as literature-supported mechanisms
- The platform is for screening, not definitive attribution
]

---

# Future Improvements

.pull-left[
### Data and workflow

- Add external elevation-change datasets
- Improve consistency of temporal comparison
- Add richer AIS or shipping-network analysis
]

.pull-right[
### Advanced analysis

- Extend toward stronger predictive modelling
- Add machine-learning classification after baseline validation
- Improve app outputs for planning and communication
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# References

## Key literature and datasets

---

# Literature and Data Sources

.pull-left[
### Key literature

- Larocca et al. (2023)
- Carrivick et al. (2023)
- Yu et al. (2022)
- Bendixen et al. (2019)
- Additional comparison literature on mining dust and Arctic risk
]

.pull-right[
### Core datasets

- GLIMS glacier outlines
- Landsat Collection 2
- Sentinel-2 SR Harmonized
- ArcticDEM Mosaic V4.1
- MODIS / VIIRS
- GEUS mineral portal
- Port / AIS datasets
- NunaGIS
- Statistics Greenland
]

---

class: center, middle, inverse

# Thank You


---
title: "Greenland Glacier Retreat Susceptibility Application"
subtitle: "A Google Earth Engine App for Cryosphere–Industry Interaction"
author: "Entao Wang, Penghao Gao, XinLei Shi, Yunjie Zhou, Siyu Chen, Ho Man Chan"
institute: "Data Dishwasher CASA0025: Building Spatial Applications with Big Data"
date: "2026/04/25 (updated: `r Sys.Date()`)"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, tools/xaringan-themer.css]
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-location: right
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: auto
    logo: images/greenland_ice.webp
    footer: "CASA0025: Building Spatial Applications with Big Data"
css: tools/style.css
---

---
title: "Greenland Glacier Retreat Susceptibility Application"
subtitle: "A Google Earth Engine App for Cryosphere–Industry Interaction"
author: "Entao Wang, Penghao Gao, XinLei Shi, Yunjie Zhou, Siyu Chen, Ho Man Chan"
institute: "Data Dishwasher CASA0025: Building Spatial Applications with Big Data"
date: "2026/04/25 (updated: `r Sys.Date()`)"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: [default, xaringan-themer.css]
    lib_dir: libs
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
    seal: false
---

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
library(fontawesome)
library(xaringanthemer)
library(xaringanExtra)
library(knitr)
```

```{r xaringan-themer, include=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
style_mono_light(
  base_color = "#1b3a4b",
  background_color = "#fafafa",
  header_font_google = google_font("Lato"),
  text_font_google   = google_font("Lato"),
  code_font_google   = google_font("Lato"),
  base_font_size = "20px",
  header_h1_font_size = "2.2rem",
  header_h2_font_size = "1.7rem",
  header_h3_font_size = "1.3rem",
  extra_css = list(
    ".remark-slide-content" = list(
      "font-size" = "20px",
      "line-height" = "1.4"
    ),
    ".title-slide" = list(
      "background-image" = "url('images/greenland_ice.webp')",
      "background-size" = "cover",
      "background-position" = "center",
      "background-repeat" = "no-repeat",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
    ),
    ".title-slide a" = list(
      "color" = "white",
      "text-decoration" = "none"
    ),
    ".title-slide a:visited" = list(
      "color" = "white"
    ),
    ".title-slide h1" = list(
      "font-size" = "44px",
      "margin-bottom" = "16px",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
    ),
    ".title-slide h2" = list(
      "font-size" = "26px",
      "margin-top" = "0px",
      "margin-bottom" = "24px",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
    ),
    ".title-slide h3" = list(
      "font-size" = "18px",
      "font-weight" = "normal",
      "margin-bottom" = "10px",
      "color" = "white",
      "text-shadow" = "none"
    )
  )
)
```

```{r tech-setup, include=FALSE}
options(htmltools.dir.version = FALSE)

style_extra_css(css = list(
   ".pull-left-wide" = list("float" = "left", "width" = "60%"),
   ".pull-right-narrow" = list("float" = "right", "width" = "35%"),
   ".pull-left-3" = list("float" = "left", "width" = "30%"),
   ".pull-middle-3" = list("float" = "left", "width" = "30%", "margin-left" = "5%", "margin-right" = "5%"),
   ".pull-right-3" = list("float" = "right", "width" = "30%"),
   ".bottom-note" = list("position" = "absolute","bottom" = "1em","left" = "1em", "right" = "1em"),
   ".clearfix" = list("clear" = "both", "display" = "block")
))
xaringanExtra::use_panelset()
```

class: center, middle, title-slide

# Greenland Glacier Retreat Susceptibility Application

## A Google Earth Engine App for Cryosphere–Industry Interaction

### Data Dishwasher

### CASA0025: Building Spatial Applications with Big Data

### (updated: `r Sys.Date()`)

---

# Project Summary

.pull-left-wide[
**What this app does**

- Builds an interactive Google Earth Engine platform for glacier retreat, climate warming, and mining activity in Greenland
- Combines remote sensing change detection, terrain analysis, anthropogenic proximity indicators, and Random Forest susceptibility modelling
- Exports computationally intensive outputs as a multi-band Earth Engine asset for a responsive app
]

.pull-right-narrow[
```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='100%', fig.align='center'}
knitr::include_graphics("images/greenland_mosaic.jpg")
```
<p style="font-size: 0.6em; text-align: center; margin-top: 0;">
Figure 1: Greenland glacier landscape
</p>
]

.clearfix[]

<div style="background: #1b3a4b; color: white; padding: 10px 16px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 0.9em;">
The platform identifies where climate, terrain, and mining-related accessibility coincide to support exploratory glacier vulnerability screening.
</div>

---

# Problem Statement

.pull-left[
### Core challenge

- Greenland's peripheral glaciers are retreating rapidly under Arctic warming
- Mining and mineral development add new pressures in fragile cryospheric environments
- Existing scientific outputs are difficult for non-specialist users to explore interactively
]

.pull-right[
### This app responds by combining

- Historical glacier retreat detection
- Warming trend analysis
- Terrain controls
- Proximity to mining, settlements, and coastlines
- Predictive retreat susceptibility modelling
]

---

# End Users

.pull-left[
### Who it is for

- Arctic environmental policymakers
- Greenland planning and resource governance agencies
- Climate and cryosphere researchers
- Mining and environmental impact analysts
- NGOs focused on Arctic sustainability
]

.pull-right[
### Why it helps

- Split-panel interface for visual comparison
- Click-based local statistics
- Model diagnostics for interpretation
- Supports both research use and policy communication
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle

## Data and interface

---

# Main App Interface

.pull-left-wide[
**Core interface design**

- Dual-map interface for comparing glacier conditions around 2000 and 2024
- Layer toggles for retreat, susceptibility, warming, mining, settlements, and topography
- Click-on-map inspector for local glacier-climate statistics
- Output panel for hotspot summaries and model diagnostics
]

.pull-right-narrow[
```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='100%', fig.align='center'}
knitr::include_graphics("images/greenland_ice_sheet.jpg")
```
<p style="font-size: 0.6em; text-align: center; margin-top: 0;">
Figure 2: Example dashboard layout for Greenland monitoring
</p>
]

.clearfix[]

---

# Key Datasets

| Theme | Main data source | Role in the app |
|------|------|------|
| Glacier mapping | Landsat 7 / 8 / 9 C2 | NDSI glacier extent and retreat detection |
| Glacier reference | GLIMS 2023 | Glacier boundary support |
| Topography | ArcticDEM v4 | Elevation and slope predictors |
| Boundary and coast | LSIB Simple | Greenland extent and coastline |
| Climate | Landsat surface temperature | Summer temperature baseline and warming trend |
| Mining | Drillholes and mineral licences | Anthropogenic proximity indicators |
| Settlements | Greenland settlements and cities | Human geography context |

---

# Predictor Layers

.pull-left[
### Environmental predictors

- Elevation
- Slope
- Early summer temperature
- Recent summer temperature
- Warming trend
]

.pull-right[
### Anthropogenic predictors

- Distance to mining drillholes
- Distance to mineral licences
- Distance to settlements
- Distance to coastline
]

---

# App Outputs

.pull-left[
### Map outputs

- Glacier extent around 2000
- Glacier extent around 2024
- Retreat mask
- Susceptibility surface
- Warming-rate surface
]

.pull-right[
### Analytical outputs

- Regional statistics in 25 km buffers
- Glacier area change
- Temperature change
- Mean elevation
- Local retreat susceptibility
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# Methodology

## Data integration and modelling workflow

---

# Workflow Overview

.panelset[
.panel[.panel-name[Step 1: Glacier Mapping]
- Use Landsat 7, 8, and 9 summer imagery
- Map glacier extent with the Normalized Difference Snow Index (NDSI)
- Build historical (~1999–2002) and recent (~2022–2024) glacier inventories
]

.panel[.panel-name[Step 2: Predictor Building]
- Derive terrain variables from ArcticDEM
- Compute climate baseline and warming trend from Landsat surface temperature
- Create proximity rasters to mines, settlements, and coastline
]

.panel[.panel-name[Step 3: Susceptibility Modelling]
- Label historical retreat and stable glacier pixels
- Train Random Forest with spatial block validation
- Export outputs as a multi-band Earth Engine asset
]
]

---

# Glacier Change Detection

.pull-left[
### Glacier mapping logic

- Historical glacier extent from Landsat summer composites
- Recent glacier extent from Landsat summer composites
- Retreat defined as ice present historically but absent recently
]

.pull-right[
### Cleaning strategy

- Remove seasonal snow noise
- Reduce cloud contamination
- Handle Landsat striping artefacts
- Apply connected-pixel patch cleaning
]

---

# Climate Trend Modelling

.pull-left[
### Temperature variables

- Early summer temperature baseline (~2000–2002)
- Recent summer temperature (~2022–2024)
- Pixel-level warming trend (2001–2024)
]

.pull-right[
### Main method

- Landsat surface temperature used as the thermal source
- Per-pixel linear fit estimates warming rate
- Warming rate expressed as degrees C per year
]

---

# Random Forest Model

.pull-left[
### Training design

- Retreat pixels as positive samples
- Stable glacier pixels as negative samples
- Ambiguous pixels excluded
]

.pull-right[
### Validation design

- Spatial block validation instead of random split
- 10 Greenland-wide spatial folds
- Folds 0–6 for training
- Folds 7–9 for testing
]

---

# Model Inputs and Outputs

.pull-left[
### Predictor stack

- Elevation
- Slope
- Warming rate
- Mean summer temperature
- Mine proximity
- Settlement proximity
- Coast proximity
]

.pull-right[
### Model outputs

- Classification model for validation
- Regression-mode Random Forest for continuous susceptibility
- Continuous susceptibility surface from 0 to 1
- Output masked to present-day glaciers
]

---

# Model Evaluation

.pull-left[
### Performance metrics

- Accuracy
- Kappa
- Precision
- Recall
- F1 Score
]

.pull-right[
### Interpretation support

- Variable importance
- Local click-based summaries
- Spatial hotspot screening
- Regional statistics around selected locations
]

---

# Precomputed Asset Architecture

.pull-left[
### Why precompute?

- Greenland-scale Landsat compositing is expensive
- Distance rasters and machine learning are computationally heavy
- Recomputing at app startup would be too slow
]

.pull-right[
### Asset stores

- Glacier extent 2000
- Glacier extent 2024
- Retreat mask
- Warming rate
- Temperature surfaces
- Susceptibility predictions
- Elevation and metadata
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# How It Works

## From user input to hotspot output

---

# App Logic

.panelset[
.panel[.panel-name[1–2]
.pull-left[
### 1. Select theme or layer

User turns on glacier, warming, susceptibility, or mining-related layers.
]

.pull-right[
### 2. Compare periods

The app displays glacier conditions around 2000 and 2024 in a dual-map layout.
]
]

.panel[.panel-name[3–4]
.pull-left[
### 3. Inspect location

Clicking a point generates regional statistics within a 25 km buffer.
]

.pull-right[
### 4. Interpret hotspot

Users assess whether high susceptibility overlaps with warming and nearby mining-related access.
]
]

.panel[.panel-name[5]
### 5. Return outputs

- Retreat maps
- Susceptibility maps
- Local buffer summaries
- Model diagnostics
]
]

---

# Example User Question

.pull-left[
### User asks

Which present-day glacier areas in Greenland appear most susceptible to future retreat, and are they near mining activity or settlements?
]

.pull-right[
### App returns

- Susceptibility surface
- Retreat history context
- Nearby mining and settlement proximity
- Local glacier-climate statistics
]

---

# Example GEE Logic

```js
var asset = ee.Image('projects/your-project/assets/greenland_susceptibility');
var glac2000 = asset.select('glacier_2000');
var glac2024 = asset.select('glacier_2024');
var retreat = asset.select('retreat');
var susc = asset.select('susceptibility');

Map.addLayer(susc, {min: 0, max: 1, palette: ['blue', 'yellow', 'red']}, 'Susceptibility');
```

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# Limitations

## What the platform can and cannot do

---

# Main Limitations

.pull-left[
### Technical

- Temporal mismatch across Landsat, ArcticDEM, and project assets
- Resolution mismatch across environmental layers
- Greenland-wide preprocessing remains computationally demanding
]

.pull-right[
### Interpretation

- Proximity does not prove causality
- Mining indicators are screening variables, not direct causal proof
- The app is an exploratory platform, not a definitive attribution tool
]

---

# Future Improvements

.pull-left[
### Data and workflow

- Add stronger elevation-change datasets
- Improve temporal consistency between predictors
- Extend mining and infrastructure datasets
]

.pull-right[
### Advanced analysis

- Improve model tuning and validation
- Add richer explanatory outputs
- Extend toward planning and governance communication
]

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# References

## Key literature and datasets

---

# Literature and Data Sources

.pull-left[
### Key literature

- Greenland glacier retreat and cryosphere change studies
- Arctic warming and peripheral glacier literature
- Spatial machine learning and validation studies
]

.pull-right[
### Core datasets

- Landsat Collection 2
- GLIMS glacier outlines
- ArcticDEM v4
- LSIB Simple boundary
- Greenland mining and settlement assets
]

---

class: center, middle, inverse

# Thank You
